---
title: "RLMHG01: Historical BAS word-reading growth, waves 1-3 (Byrne et al.)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

RLMHG01 is the **first historical, non-intervention model** in the package and the
first to run on a second study (`study_id="rlm"`): the Byrne, MacDonald & Buckley
reading-language-memory cohort. It estimates how BAS word-reading scores grow over
the first three annual waves for three reading groups (Down syndrome, average
readers, reading-matched), on the complete-case subset that reproduces the paper's
Table 2.

::: {.callout-important title="Descriptive natural-history, NOT an intervention effect"}

`readgrp` is a **cohort factor**, not a randomised or assigned condition, and this
model carries no treatment, baseline-as-precision, or adjustment-set structure.
Every quantity here is a **descriptive** group-by-wave expected score or growth
rate — natural-history evidence, not a causal effect. The intervention-effect
estimands live in the RLI (`lrp...`) suite.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="Audit baseline"}

The model is fit on the per-measure complete-case subset (subjects with BAS word
reading observed at all three waves). The observed group-by-wave means reproduce
the paper's Table 2 complete-case figures and are written beside the posterior
summaries as the audit baseline. The 96-vs-97-row provenance question flagged in
the data README is unresolved and out of scope here.

:::

## Model

For child $i$ in reading group $g[i]$ at wave $t$, the bounded BAS word-reading
count (denominator $N = 87$) is Beta-Binomial with an age-free, group-by-wave
population mean and a stable per-child offset:

$$
\text{score}_{it} \sim \mathrm{BetaBinomial}(N,\ p_{it},\ \kappa),
\qquad
\operatorname{logit}(p_{it}) = \eta_{g[i],\,t} + u_i .
$$

$\eta_{g,t}$ is the population group-by-wave expected score (a $3 \times 3$ grid);
$u_i$ is a non-centred, **group-centred** per-child random intercept
($u_i = (z_i - \bar z_{g[i]})\,\sigma_{\text{subject}}$) so the group-by-wave level
$\eta$ carries the group mean; $\kappa$ is measurement overdispersion. Deterministic
quantities report the expected item score per group and wave, within-group growth
across the two annual intervals, and pairwise total-growth (wave 1 to wave 3)
contrasts between groups.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_historical_growth.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
